name: Ponte Caldelas - return via Coto Aguda and Tourón Archaeological Area
name_original: Ponte Caldelas - vuelta por Coto Aguda y Área Arqueológica de Tourón
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ponte Caldelas offers diverse terrain, characterized by a mix of natural monuments, serene lakes, and challenging mountain peaks. The region provides a range of cycling routes suitable for various skill levels. Riders can explore varied Galician landscapes, including forests and river environments. This area is part of the broader Pontevedra province, known for its extensive network of mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Ponte Caldelas The most popular mountain bike trail is Ponte Caldelas - return via Coto Aguda and Tourón Archaeological Area, a 17.7 miles (28.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through varied landscapes including the Tourón Archaeological Area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alto Monte Xaxán – Lake Cotorredondo loop from Pontevedra-Universidade, a difficult 31.0 miles (49.9 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation changes and views of Lake Cotorredondo. Local mountain bikers also love the Community of Montes Fountain – Ponte do Burgo loop from Pontevedra, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Mountain biking around Ponte Caldelas is defined by diverse terrain, natural monuments, and mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Ponte Caldelas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ponte Caldelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ponte Caldelas?
A: There are over 130 mountain bike trails around Ponte Caldelas, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ponte Caldelas?
A: Ponte Caldelas, located in Galicia, Spain, offers diverse terrain that can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. While the Río Tomeza Riverside Trail can be prone to flooding during the rainy season, many trails remain accessible. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and conditions, though winter can also offer unique riding experiences.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ponte Caldelas?
A: Yes, Ponte Caldelas offers 14 easy mountain bike trails. These routes are ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride through the scenic Galician landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by waterfalls in Ponte Caldelas?
A: Yes, you can find trails that include waterfalls. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Pulpo Inn – Barosa Waterfall loop from Pontevedra-Universidade&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 44.7 km long, passes by the Barosa Waterfall.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Ponte Caldelas?
A: The trails around Ponte Caldelas feature a mix of natural monuments, serene lakes, and mountain peaks. You can encounter attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1400940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1449456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eiras Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/738182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontillón do Castro Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural monuments include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1620962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulló Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1503550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte das Sete Fontes, Monte Requeiro Rest Area&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 38 difficult mountain bike trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Monte Xaxán – Lake Cotorredondo loop from Pontevedra-Universidade&lt;/a&gt;, a 49.9 km route with significant elevation changes, or the even longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo – Cruz de Paralaia loop from Pontevedra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 64.3 km and features over 1500 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ponte Caldelas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ponte Caldelas are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from forests and river environments to challenging mountain peaks, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that include historical or archaeological sites?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. For instance, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980652733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Caldelas - return via Coto Aguda and Tourón Archaeological Area&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.6 km loop, passes through the significant Tourón Archaeological Area.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the Ponte Caldelas area?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Ponte Caldelas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14938181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Community of Montes Fountain – Ponte do Burgo loop from Pontevedra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Monte Xaxán – Lake Cotorredondo loop from Pontevedra-Universidade&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Ponte Caldelas?
A: Given the region's mountainous terrain and diverse landscapes, many trails offer rewarding viewpoints. Routes that feature significant elevation changes, such as those around Monte Xaxán or Cruz de Paralaia, are likely to provide panoramic views of the surrounding Galician countryside and lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1400940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike tours in Ponte Caldelas?
A: The duration of mountain bike tours varies significantly based on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980652733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Caldelas - return via Coto Aguda and Tourón Archaeological Area&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 50 minutes, while more challenging and longer routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo – Cruz de Paralaia loop from Pontevedra&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to over 6 hours.
tips: Open-air archaeological site displaying many examples of Iron Age petroglyph rock art created 3000 years ago by early Celtic settlers on the Galician coast

From the detour to the indicator sign, it is easy to climb, but the final part is very broken and it is very difficult (I think almost impossible) to pedal to the viewpoint.
The windows of the (abandoned) guardhouse are not closed, so, in case of bad weather, you can take refuge inside. On the ground floor there is a small bathroom, and the railing to climb to the top is in poor condition.
Small town on the river Verdugo that flows into the Ría de Vigo. A good place to have lunch (and a nice glass of wine) and rest by the river before heading home
